title: Codonoboea angustifolia
taxonomy: Codonoboea angustifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the 'African Violet or Gesneriad family'. The Gesneriaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Codonoboea angustifolia is a type of epiphytic plant, meaning it grows on other plants for support. The classification of Codonoboea angustifolia is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida.

intro: Codonoboea angustifolia, also known as the "Asian cat's claw vine," is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Gesneriaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Codonoboea angustifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess antioxidant and free radical scavenging activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and infusions.
